assignment: Repair replay operability and resolve the substantive signal-exit classification ambiguity without changing the bounded signal-only API unless executable evidence rejects it.
required_checks: Write all v3 artifacts in workers/dkr-2-v3; never overwrite prior evidence.
Use a repo-root-resolvable replay command beginning with bash and verify it from the feature worktree root.
Probe cancellation where the child rejects with signal.reason, a canonical AbortError, and an unrelated original error after the signal has aborted.
Pin the rule: cancellation classification must not hide an unrelated original error. State and replay the exact accepted identity/name rule.

Replay scalar child, scalar error, stream normal, producer error, consumer break, stream close, three-level nested close, sibling isolation, uncooperative scalar waiting, original-error preservation, and unhandled rejections.
Replay close order: abort descendants, return active stream iterators, join descendants, then clean parent resources.
Keep optional caller signal plus ExecutionContext.signal and no public cancel only if all caller-coverage probes pass.
Create a complete v3 checkpoint with resolvable hashes and numeric walls; do not self-accept.
forbidden: Product code, tests, docs, manifests, git history, PR state, external services, frame, tree, or another worker's artifacts
Public cancel, start, spawn, task handles, worker pools, session-specific Lite semantics, error swallowing, or wall weakening
required_outputs: workers/dkr-2-v3/progress.jsonl via the absolute OKRA store helper worker-report command
terminal_condition: Finish when all behavior and API cases replay from the repository root, signal classification preserves unrelated errors, and independent validation is the only proof gate.
